profile.behavior: Forages for seeds and insects in low vegetation; males sing from exposed high perches and may swish the tail while perched. Migratory movements are chiefly nocturnal.
field_marks: Small, stocky, sparrow-sized bunting with a short tail.
Adult breeding male is vivid indigo-blue overall, often richer blue on the head.
Short, thick, conical bill; silver-gray in breeding males.
Dark eye and dark legs contrast subtly with the blue plumage.
Wings and tail are darker blue-black, with compact rounded proportions.
Females and immatures are brownish with a pale throat, faint breast streaking, and occasional blue on wing, tail, or rump.
profile.habitat: Brushy, weedy edge habitats: hedgerows, overgrown fields and pastures, woodland margins, clearings, and shrubby vegetation near open ground.

sources.title: Indigo Bunting Identification — All About Birds

findings: The illustrated male has one head, one beak, two anatomically normal legs and feet, two wings (the far wing is occluded by the side-on pose), and one tail; no duplicated or malformed anatomy is present.

Plumage, silver-gray conical bill, dark eye and legs, darker flight feathers, compact body, and rounded wing/tail treatment agree with the supplied male reference photographs and live species accounts.
Minor illustration variance: the bill is drawn somewhat larger/deeper and the tail slightly more elongate than in the supplied photographs; neither is a material misidentification.

All visible plate claims were checked. The stated measurements exactly match All About Birds; Audubon gives a slightly broader maximum length (5.5 in / 14 cm), so the plate's length is a valid narrower published range. No factual text error found.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.

verification_sources.title: Indigo Bunting Identification — All About Birds
